user_query:
I’m onboarding a new autonomous assistant for my team and I want a durable memory setup from day one. Please read the following files under input/:
- input/agent_brief.json (draft of the assistant’s persona and mission)
- input/user_profile.json (my details, preferences, timezone, and constraints)
- input/existing_notes.md (messy notes from prior planning sessions, including corrections and conflicting decisions you must reconcile)
- input/session_date.txt (the ISO date to use for today’s daily note)

Using those, scaffold a complete 5-file agent memory architecture and supporting files under output/ with the following deliverables:
1) Core files at workspace root:
   - output/SOUL.md (sacred identity — include who the agent is, core mission, operating traits, standards, and hard boundaries; clearly mark it as sacred and not to be shared externally)
   - output/IDENTITY.md (business card with Name, Creature, Vibe, Emoji)
   - output/USER.md (my details, communication preferences, current goals, timezone, and a "Never Assume" section)
   - output/AGENTS.md (operating manual including: an explicit Session Start read order; Memory Rules that include the exact WAL protocol string "STOP → WRITE → THEN RESPOND"; typed memory entries convention; and an Always-Search protocol line that says "Always run memory_search before answering about prior context"; also include security boundaries for sacred files)
   - output/MEMORY.md (curated long-term memory with typed entries — at minimum include a [DECISION] and a [PREFERENCE], and at least one additional typed entry; resolve contradictions found in input/existing_notes.md by preferring the later-dated decision and note the reversal in the newer entry with phrasing like "(reverses <date> decision)")
   - output/TOOLS.md (environment-specific notes relevant to this workspace, if any)
   - output/HEARTBEAT.md (a short checklist the agent can use during periodic heartbeats; keep it small)

2) Memory directory:
   - output/memory/decisions.md (active corrections and redirects written as typed entries — extract at least one concrete correction or preference from input/existing_notes.md)
   - output/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md (today’s daily raw log — use the exact date from input/session_date.txt as the filename; include brief bullets of what you set up today)
   - At least one prose-as-title topic file under output/memory/ that captures a key claim or decision you extracted (e.g., a decision about auth flow). The file must:
     - Use a kebab-case, claim-style filename (not a category name)
     - Start with YAML frontmatter containing a summary list and an updated date, e.g.:
       ---
       summary:
         - Key decision or claim
         - Current status
         - Actionable or archived
       updated: YYYY-MM-DD
       ---
     - Provide brief supporting notes below the frontmatter

Additional requirements:
- Use typed memory entries exactly in the form: [TYPE] YYYY-MM-DD: content (where TYPE is one of DECISION, PREFERENCE, FACT, ENTITY, EPISODE, LESSON, AGENT_IDENTITY).
- Apply contradiction detection on the notes in input/existing_notes.md. If you find conflicting decisions (e.g., an older decision to use OAuth2 implicit grant and a newer decision to use PKCE), the newer decision must win. Reflect that in output/MEMORY.md with a reversal note and create a corresponding prose-as-title topic file about the final decision.
- Enforce security boundaries: never instruct to share SOUL.md, USER.md, or MEMORY.md externally, and state in AGENTS.md that MEMORY.md is only loaded in main 1:1 sessions.
- Do not invent facts; only extract what you can justify from input/.

Deliver all artifacts under output/ exactly at the listed paths.
